agentskit-chat.purpose: How Ask Playbook composes the public AgentsKit Chat framework without recreating chat state.

contributing.purpose: Turn a reproducible engineering lesson into a reviewable Playbook pattern, prompt, template, or gate.

discovery.purpose: See which Playbook questions answer instantly in the browser and when repository-specific synthesis uses the backend.

for-agents.purpose: The shortest reliable path for an agent to retrieve, apply, and verify the Agents Playbook.

getting-started.purpose: Adopt the playbook in your project in under an hour.

glossary.purpose: Short definitions for the terms used across this playbook.

playbook.purpose: The open engineering harness for coding agents — train repeatable behavior, not model weights.

matrix.purpose: This is the master content map. Each cell points to the practices that apply when pillar meets SDLC phase.

onboard-your-agent.purpose: One paste that feeds any coding agent the whole playbook and has it audit your repo — Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Codex, Copilot, and more.

phases--01-discover.purpose: Define what you are building, who consumes it, and what success looks like — before agents touch the codebase.

phases--02-design.purpose: Turn the discover brief into ADRs, RFCs, and a contract package skeleton the build phase can compose against.

phases--03-build.purpose: Where most of the agent-augmented work happens. Discipline shifts from ''writing code'' to ''stating intent, then verifying output against it''.

phases--04-test.purpose: How ''tests pass'' stops being a feeling and starts being a contract.

phases--05-ship.purpose: How to turn a green main into a release without surprising consumers.

phases--06-operate.purpose: What ''running it'' looks like after agents have shipped the first release.

agent-compatibility-pattern.purpose: One playbook, every agent — how to map the bootstrap-doc convention onto Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, Codex, and the rest.

bootstrap-doc-pattern.purpose: The file an agent reads first, every session. Two files together: one for non-negotiables, one for routing.

concurrent-agent-pattern.purpose: How to survive — and benefit from — multiple agents working in the same repo at the same time.

context-management-pattern.purpose: Engineer what goes into the context window — selection, ordering, compaction, retention — because the context is the program the model runs.

hallucination-reduction-pattern.purpose: Make model output you can put your name on — grounding, constrained generation, verification, and abstention — so the system says "I don''t know" instead of confidently inventing.

honest-confidence-pattern.purpose: How to report done-ness so a human can trust it — separating what was automatically verified from what was merely claimed.

human-in-the-loop-pattern.purpose: Design where humans review, approve, correct, and take over from agents — so autonomy scales with confidence and every correction becomes training signal.

pillars--ai-collaboration.purpose: How to make an agent productive in your repo on day one and durably good across sessions.

memory-pattern.purpose: How to make agents durably learn from prior sessions without polluting context with chat transcripts.

open-knowledge-format-pattern.purpose: Represent agent-readable knowledge as a directory of markdown + YAML frontmatter — the OKF open standard, and how it formalizes patterns this playbook already uses.

prompt-versioning-pattern.purpose: Treat prompts as versioned, tested, experimentable production assets — not strings buried in code — so you can A/B them, measure impact, and roll back instantly.

self-describe-pattern.purpose: Ship a machine-readable manifest of what your system can do, so external agents can discover and call it without reading your source.

slash-commands-pattern.purpose: How to turn repeated workflows into palette-invoked commands so they run identically every time.

sub-agent-pattern.purpose: How to delegate scoped tasks to specialist agents so the orchestrator stays focused.

tool-design-pattern.purpose: Design the abstraction layer between a model and your product — tools, file systems, artifacts, skills — so complex multi-step work feels natural to the model and stays reliable for users.

pillars--ai-collaboration--universal.purpose: How to get production-grade work out of an AI coding agent, durably, across sessions and across multiple agents working in parallel.

verify-first-pattern.purpose: Why an agent must trust live code over docs, plans, and its own memory — and how to verify before claiming or "fixing".

adr-pattern.purpose: How to record architecture decisions so future agents (and humans) can find them, trust them, and supersede them cleanly.

anti-overengineering.purpose: How to keep agents (and engineers) from building three layers of abstraction for what would have been ten lines of code.

api-gateway-pattern.purpose: The edge between clients and your services — what belongs there, what doesn''t, and how to keep it from becoming a monolith in disguise.

api-versioning-pattern.purpose: How to evolve public contracts without surprising consumers.

caching-cdn-pattern.purpose: How to layer caches so the system is fast, cheap, and consistent — three properties at tension.

contracts-zod-pattern.purpose: TS-concrete recipe for a typed JSON-RPC / HTTP / IPC boundary. Scales to several hundred methods across dozens of namespaces in a real production codebase.

distributed-data-pattern.purpose: How to design data layout when one database stops being enough — read replicas, sharding, replication lag, CAP trade-offs, eventual consistency.

error-hierarchy.purpose: How to design an error model that survives multi-agent development and client-side pattern matching.

event-streaming-pattern.purpose: How to design async, decoupled communication via queues, pub/sub, and event streams — without losing events, double-processing, or stalling consumers.

feature-flags-pattern.purpose: How to ship code separately from shipping behavior, without accumulating a flag graveyard.

file-size-budget.purpose: How to keep files reviewable when agents write most of the code.

iac-pattern.purpose: How to define + version + review + apply infrastructure as code, instead of clicking around cloud consoles.

pillars--architecture.purpose: How to keep a codebase legible and modular under multi-agent development.

multi-region-pattern.purpose: How to operate across geographic regions for latency, availability, and data sovereignty — without losing your mind.

offline-first-sync-pattern.purpose: How to design apps that work without network connectivity and reconcile state when connectivity returns.

platform-engineering-idp-pattern.purpose: How to build the layer between cloud / infra and product engineers, so product teams ship fast without re-learning infra each time.

rfc-pattern.purpose: When the decision is bigger than your team. Used for changes to public contracts, wire formats, plugin protocols, and any breaking change a consumer outside this repo would notice.

service-mesh-pattern.purpose: How to handle cross-service communication concerns (mTLS, retries, observability, routing) without coding them in every service.

ts-concrete.purpose: Copy-paste-ready recipes that implement [`universal.md`](/docs/pillars/architecture/universal) on a TypeScript stack. Calibrated on a real multi-package, multi-app monorepo built primarily by AI agents over ~1 year.

pillars--architecture--universal.purpose: Stack-agnostic. Applies to any language, any framework.

pillars--governance.purpose: How multiple agents (and humans) coordinate work in one repo without subtracting each other''s progress.

merge-rules-pattern.purpose: How to resolve conflicts so the merge sums work instead of subtracting it.

phased-pr-pattern.purpose: How to ship initiatives too big for one PR without ending up with a long-lived branch hell.

pr-intent-pattern.purpose: The manifest that makes a PR''s claims verifiable.

tombstone-pattern.purpose: How to retire a doc, plan, ADR, screen, or package without losing the trail.

pillars--governance--universal.purpose: How multiple contributors — agents and humans — coordinate so the whole sums.

adversarial-bug-hunt-pattern.purpose: How to find real logic bugs with a find → refute → reproduce loop, instead of trusting a single agent that says "looks fine".

agent-eval-framework-pattern.purpose: How to measure whether an AI agent is actually good — deterministic checks, LLM-as-judge, and production monitoring — so quality scales with the system instead of breaking as it grows.

alerting-runbooks-pattern.purpose: How to turn observability data into a healthy alerting setup — pages that matter, runbooks that work, alert hygiene that prevents pager fatigue.

chaos-engineering-pattern.purpose: How to find failure modes before customers do — deliberately, in a controlled way.

ci-cd-pipeline-pattern.purpose: How to wire continuous integration + continuous delivery so the path from commit to production is short, verifiable, reversible, and boring.

contract-testing-pattern.purpose: How to verify that consumer + provider agree on a contract, without expensive end-to-end tests.

cost-optimization-pattern.purpose: How to control cloud spend without micromanaging every commit.

fail-loud-defaults-pattern.purpose: Why a silent no-op default plus a test harness that overrides it lets broken production wiring pass CI green.

pillars--quality.purpose: How to know the code works without manually reviewing every agent-produced diff.

mutation-testing-pattern.purpose: How to score whether your unit tests actually catch bugs, beyond what coverage tells you.

observability-pattern.purpose: How to know what the system is doing in production, beyond ''tests passed''.

performance-budgets-pattern.purpose: How to keep ''it''s fast enough'' from drifting into ''why is it slow?''

pre-push-pattern.purpose: The safety net between local changes and CI.

product-analytics-experimentation-patter.purpose: How to measure what users do, run experiments cleanly, and let data drive product decisions — without inviting bias or noise.

property-fuzz-testing-pattern.purpose: How to test the laws your code must obey, instead of guessing individual inputs — and how this catches crypto and parser bugs example tests never will.

quality-gates-pattern.purpose: How to bundle structural rules into one fast command an agent runs before every push.

sanity-pattern.purpose: The cross-cutting audit that catches what individual gates miss.

test-pyramid.purpose: How to mix test types so the cheap ones catch most bugs and the expensive ones cover what only they can.

pillars--quality--universal.purpose: How to know agent-produced code works without manually reading every diff.

ai-llm-safety-pattern.purpose: How to ship LLM-powered features without prompt injection, jailbreaks, data leakage, hallucinated tools, or runaway costs.

audit-ledger-pattern.purpose: How to prove who did what when, in a way that survives compromise.

compliance-framework-pattern.purpose: How to map SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / GDPR / HIPAA controls onto your codebase + ops, so audits are evidence-driven, not theatre.

container-k8s-security-pattern.purpose: How to ship containers + run them on Kubernetes without making the runtime an attack surface.

data-classification-pattern.purpose: How to label every field by sensitivity, then make the labels drive redaction, retention, residency, and access.

dependency-hygiene-pattern.purpose: How to keep your dependency tree healthy across thousands of transitive packages without it becoming a full-time job.

egress-firewall-pattern.purpose: Default-deny outbound network access, configurable per workspace, evaluable at every call site.

governance-posture-pattern.purpose: Expose the system''s live security and governance posture as a read-only, machine-readable signal — so agents, dashboards, and auditors can see what is enforced without admin rights.

pillars--security.purpose: How to build security in from day one when the people writing the code are agents, not a hardened security team.

multi-tenant-isolation-pattern.purpose: How to host many customers in one system without one customer leaking, blocking, or paying for another.

on-call-rotation-pattern.purpose: How to keep a system reliable 24/7 without burning out the team that maintains it.

rate-limiting-ddos-pattern.purpose: How to absorb traffic spikes — both benign (popular feature) and malicious (DDoS / abuse) — without breaking for legitimate users.

rbac-pattern.purpose: How to model ''who can do what'' so the checks survive multi-agent edits.

secrets-leak-postmortem-playbook.purpose: When a secret leaks — git history, log, screenshot, public artefact — what to do, in what order, within what timeline.

secrets-mgmt-deep-pattern.purpose: Beyond `vault-pattern.md` — operational specifics: dynamic secrets, short-lived credentials, secret-zero, secret-less architectures, OIDC federation.

session-mgmt-pattern.purpose: How to model + protect user sessions — login state, refresh, revocation, idle expiry, device binding.

threat-model-template.purpose: Skeleton for the per-project threat model doc. Lives at `docs/security/threat-model.md`. Revisited every release.

pillars--security--universal.purpose: How to bake security into the codebase from PR #1, when agents (not a hardened security team) write most of it.

vault-pattern.purpose: How to store secrets so a leaked log line, a stolen disk, or a curious agent does not compromise the system.

vulnerability-mgmt-pattern.purpose: How to keep the system patched without bottlenecking on a person watching CVE feeds.

a11y-checklist.purpose: What every shipped surface passes before merge, and how to prove it.

accessibility-deep-pattern.purpose: Beyond the per-PR checklist — the substance of WCAG-AA conformance, the failure modes that matter, the testing discipline that catches what automation cannot.

design-system-governance-pattern.purpose: How to keep a design system useful as it grows — without bloat, without dictatorial restrictions, without drift.

design-tokens-pattern.purpose: How to define visual values once and let them flow through every screen, theme, and brand.

empty-states-pattern.purpose: How to turn empty surfaces from dead ends into next-step launchpads.

i18n-deep-pattern.purpose: Beyond ''wrap every string in `t()`'' — the substance of locale-correct UI: plural rules, gender, ICU formatting, RTL, dates, numbers, currency, sorting, search.

pillars--ui-ux.purpose: How to keep a multi-screen product visually coherent, intl-ready, and accessible when agents are writing screens in parallel.

intl-pattern.purpose: How to never ship hardcoded user-visible strings, so the next locale is configuration, not a sweep.

primitives-pattern.purpose: How to ship a one-package primitives catalog so every screen looks like the same product.

pillars--ui-ux--universal.purpose: How to keep a multi-screen product visually coherent, intl-ready, accessible, and honest when agents author screens in parallel.

whitelabel-pattern.purpose: How to make every product surface reskinnable per tenant — even if you do not sell whitelabel today.

prompts.purpose: System prompts, sub-agent recipes, and slash-command bodies that consistently produce gold-standard output.

slash-clear.purpose: Reset session context cleanly without losing persistent memory.

slash-goal.purpose: Set a session goal with an explicit exit condition. Stops only when condition holds.

slash-loop.purpose: Run a task repeatedly. Either on a fixed interval or self-paced based on an exit condition.

slash-review.purpose: Multi-agent code review pass on a PR (or current branch).

slash-sanity.purpose: Run the cross-cutting sanity audit, surface drift.

slash-ship.purpose: Run the release-gate checklist. Does not actually release — produces the readiness verdict.

subagent-code-explorer.purpose: Deep-trace agent. Maps execution paths, dependencies, and abstraction layers across a feature so the orchestrator can reason about a change.

subagent-code-reviewer.purpose: Confidence-filtered review pass on a diff. Returns issues; does not approve / merge.

subagent-explore.purpose: Read-only fan-out search agent. Orchestrator delegates ''find me X'' tasks here.

subagent-plan.purpose: Designs a step-by-step implementation plan from a task description and codebase context. Does not implement.

system-architect.purpose: Inject as system prompt when the task is designing a new package boundary, new contract, new ADR, or evaluating a structural change.

system-implementer.purpose: Inject as system prompt when the task is building a sub-unit against a finalised plan.

system-reviewer.purpose: Inject as system prompt for code review passes. Produces confidence-scored issue lists, not pile-on critique.

system-security.purpose: Inject as system prompt for security-focused review passes. Narrower than the general reviewer; deeper on the threat model.

scripts.purpose: Drop-in reference implementations for the quality + structural gates the pillars rely on. Adapt to your stack; the contract each gate enforces is more important than the specific code.

ADR.template.purpose: Skeleton for an Architecture Decision Record. Copy the block below into docs/adr/NNNN-<slug>.md and fill the bracketed parts.

AGENTS.md.template.purpose: Drop at repo root. The routing table that answers ''which package owns X?'' before an agent makes the wrong edit.

CLAUDE.md.template.purpose: Drop this at the repo root as `CLAUDE.md` (or `AGENTS.md` for tool-neutral). Customise the marked sections. Keep it under 200 lines; agents read the whole thing every session.

MEMORY.md.template.purpose: Persistent agent memory survives between sessions. The pattern: one fact per file, plus an index file.

PR-intent.template.purpose: Embed this block in every PR description. A gate parses it and verifies that the diff matches the claims.

RFC.template.purpose: Skeleton for a Request for Comments. Copy the block below into docs/rfc/NNNN-<slug>.md and fill the bracketed parts.

templates.purpose: Copy-paste skeletons for the documents the playbook relies on — bootstrap docs, decision records, and the PR-intent manifest.

llmsTxt.preamble: Agents Playbook documents production practices for software built with AI coding agents.
